name: Alberta Hunter
description: American blues singer, songwriter, and nurse (1895-1984)
abstract: Alberta Hunter (April 1, 1895 – October 17, 1984) was an American jazz and blues singer and songwriter from the early 1920s to the late 1950s. After twenty years of working as a nurse, Hunter resumed her singing career in 1977.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Salute to American Song

works_created.t: He's Got a Punch Like Joe Louis

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: I went to the union the other day to get a record check, a residual from something that I did with Alberta Hunter

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: And then in the early 70s he had Alberta Hunter made her comeback at this club, and Teddy Wilson played there, and all kinds of people. Well my parents took me down to see Teddy Wilson one night...
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Alberta Hunter was the first one to sing our song, 'Someday Sweetheart.' ... Alberta Hunter was on it, and she made the first recording of 'Someday Sweetheart,' ... Alberta made it

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Wilber
about_quotes.evidence: So we took it to Alberta and set it up in the dressing room and we played the original one in G flat, and then the new one in A flat. “Oh that’s it, that’s the one.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Wilber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Whaley
about_quotes.evidence: I wrote some of her numbers. She used to come to the house and I used to write for her... I used to put it down and orchestrate it for her

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: The 3rd of July, I had another concert at the Ecole Normale de Musique. Alberta Hunter, the blues singer... were the other participants
